questions.q: What are the three colours of the Indian national flag, from top to bottom?
questions.explanation: The Indian tricolour has saffron at the top, white in the middle, and green at the bottom.

questions.q: What is the symbol at the centre of the Indian national flag?
questions.explanation: The flag bears the Ashoka Chakra, a navy-blue wheel with 24 spokes, at its centre.

questions.q: In which year was the present design of the Indian tricolour adopted?
questions.explanation: The national flag in its present form was adopted in 1947, shortly before independence.

questions.q: What colour is the central wheel on the Indian flag?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra in the centre of the flag is navy blue.

questions.q: The central wheel of the Indian flag is derived from which ancient source?
options: The Lion Capital of Ashoka at Sarnath
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra is taken from the wheel on the Lion Capital of Ashoka at Sarnath.

questions.q: Which band of the flag occupies its middle stripe?
questions.explanation: The white band lies in the middle of the tricolour, between saffron and green.

questions.q: How many spokes does the wheel at the centre of the Indian flag have?
questions.explanation: The Ashoka Chakra has 24 spokes.

questions.q: The Indian national flag is commonly known by which Hindi name?
questions.explanation: The flag is popularly called the Tiranga, meaning tricolour.

questions.q: Which earlier symbol appeared at the centre of the Congress flag before the present wheel?
questions.explanation: Earlier nationalist flags carried Gandhi's spinning wheel, the charkha, at the centre.

questions.q: On which occasion is the national flag hoisted by the Prime Minister from the Red Fort each year?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister hoists the flag from the Red Fort each Independence Day, 15 August.

questions.q: On what date in 1947 did the Constituent Assembly adopt the national flag?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the national flag on 22 July 1947.

questions.q: Who is generally credited with designing the basic flag from which the national tricolour evolved?
questions.explanation: Pingali Venkayya is widely credited with designing the flag that formed the basis of the national tricolour.

questions.q: What replaced the spinning wheel at the centre when the national flag was finalised in 1947?
questions.explanation: When the flag was adopted in 1947, the charkha was replaced by the Ashoka Chakra at the centre.

questions.q: An early flag associated with the Swadeshi era, the Calcutta flag of 1906, had how many horizontal bands?
questions.explanation: The Calcutta flag of 1906 had three horizontal bands of orange, yellow and green.

questions.q: Madame Bhikaji Cama unfurled an early Indian flag abroad in 1907 at a congress held in which city?
questions.explanation: Bhikaji Cama unfurled an early version of an Indian flag at the International Socialist Congress in Stuttgart in 1907.

questions.q: Which body adopted a flag bearing the charkha as an official Congress flag in 1931?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress formally adopted a tricolour with the charkha as its flag in 1931.

questions.q: In the official interpretation, what does the saffron band of the flag broadly signify?
questions.explanation: Saffron is commonly taken to signify strength, courage and sacrifice.

questions.q: In the official interpretation, the green band of the flag is broadly associated with what?
questions.explanation: Green is associated with fertility, growth and the auspiciousness of the land.

questions.q: Pingali Venkayya's flag concept was discussed with which leader before being refined for national use?
questions.explanation: Pingali Venkayya developed his flag ideas in consultation with Mahatma Gandhi during the 1920s.

questions.q: The committee set up in 1947 to recommend a national flag was chaired by which leader?
questions.explanation: An ad hoc flag committee headed by Rajendra Prasad recommended the national flag in 1947.

questions.q: What is the prescribed ratio of the length to the height of the Indian national flag?
questions.explanation: The national flag has a length-to-height ratio of 3:2.

questions.q: In the white band, what does the wheel symbolise in the words of those who explained the design?
questions.explanation: The wheel was explained as a symbol of motion, signifying that there is life in movement and death in stagnation.

questions.q: What material was traditionally specified for the manufacture of the official Indian national flag?
questions.explanation: The flag code traditionally required the national flag to be made of khadi, the handspun handwoven cloth.

questions.q: A 'Swaraj flag' designed in 1921 carried which arrangement before being modified?
options: Saffron and blue bands with a star
Red and green bands with a charkha
Black and white bands with a wheel
Yellow and purple bands with a lotus
questions.explanation: The 1921 design first had red and green bands with a charkha; a white band was later added on Gandhi's suggestion.

questions.q: Why was a white band added to the earlier two-colour Swaraj flag?
options: To represent other communities and as a background for the wheel
To mark the salt of the sea
questions.explanation: The white band was added to represent communities beyond the two colours and to provide a background for the charkha.

questions.q: The Ashoka Chakra adopted in 1947 is also referred to by which name linked to Buddhist tradition?
questions.explanation: The 24-spoke wheel is identified with the Dharma Chakra, the wheel of law, from the Sarnath pillar.

questions.q: Which national institution holds the rights for the standardised manufacture of the official flag in Karnataka?
options: The Khadi Development and Village Industries body at Hubli
The Survey of India at Dehradun
questions.explanation: A khadi institution at Hubli (Garag) in Karnataka has been the recognised manufacturer of the standardised national flag.

questions.q: When Pingali Venkayya first presented flag ideas, in which decade did this early work begin?
questions.explanation: Pingali Venkayya researched and proposed flag designs from the 1910s into the early 1920s.

questions.q: In 2021-2022 a campaign encouraging citizens to fly the flag at home was promoted under which name?
questions.explanation: The 'Har Ghar Tiranga' campaign encouraged people to hoist the national flag at their homes.

questions.q: The flag finally adopted in 1947 kept the same three colours as the Congress flag but changed mainly which feature?
options: The central emblem from charkha to chakra
The shape from rectangular to square
The colours from saffron-white-green to red-white-blue
questions.explanation: The principal change in 1947 was replacing the central charkha with the Ashoka Chakra, while the three colours remained.
